A.text: Electric field lines can cross in space.

B.text: The electric field lines around plate 2 exhibit a convergent pattern.

C.text: Between the two plates, the electric field lines point from plate 2 toward plate 1.

D.text: The electric field lines around plate 1 exhibit a divergent pattern.

parsed.answer_value: The electric field lines around plate 1 exhibit a divergent pattern.
parsed.rationale: Observing the visualization, the electric field lines originating from Metal Plate 1 spread outwards in all directions, which is the definition of a divergent pattern. In contrast, the lines around Metal Plate 2 converge towards it, making option B incorrect for plate 1. The arrows on the lines between the plates clearly point from plate 1 to plate 2, contradicting option C. Furthermore, electric field lines never cross each other in space, as this would imply two different field directions at a single point, invalidating option A.
